text: The Registration module shall provide an interface for citizens to register complaints with the police.
The Registration module shall facilitate information exchange between police and complainants.
The Investigation module shall automate tasks performed after initial registration entries to facilitate the investigation process.
The Prosecution module shall provide a platform for police personnel to record entries of court interactions.
The Search module shall allow police personnel to execute basic and advanced searches on cases using criteria including person, type of crime, modus operandi, and property.
The Search module shall allow users to customize result views by criminal, accused, or case.
The Citizen Interface module shall provide a conduit for information exchange and acknowledgements between citizens and police units.
The Navigation module shall provide role-based landing pages reflecting assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks.
The Configuration module shall allow authorized users to create, update, and delete state-specific data including acts, sections, castes, tribes, and property information.
The system shall provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all user interfaces in both offline and online modes.
The system shall provide an interface for users to log and track defects or enhancement requests.
The system shall send automated alerts via email or SMS to the user when actions are taken on reported alerts.
The system shall provide help-desk users with reports on defects categorized by status and age.
The system shall maintain an unalterable audit trail capturing all create, read, update, and delete actions on critical entities including cases, suspects, and property.
The audit trail shall automatically record the identity of the initiating user and the date and time of the event.
The system shall maintain audit trail data for at least the life of the case to which it refers.
The system shall allow authorized users to export audit trails for specified cases without affecting the stored data.
The system shall capture and store unauthorized access attempts in the audit trail.
The system shall provide audit reports organized by case, user, and chronological sequence.
The system shall restrict case access to specified users or user groups.
The system shall implement role-based access control for all internal functionalities.
The system shall restrict the setup of user profiles and group allocations to administrative users only.
The system shall restrict changes to security attributes and password management to super-users only.
The system shall exclude records from search results if the user does not have the required access rights.
The system shall provide meaningful error messages accompanied by explanatory text and suggested user actions.
The system shall employ a consistent set of user interface rules to provide a common look and feel.
The user interface shall be compatible with specialist software and guidelines for users with special needs.
The system shall support user-definable persistent defaults for data entry fields.
The system shall allow users to customize the graphical user interface including menus, screen layouts, colors, and fonts.
The user interface shall comply with ISO 9241 standards for ergonomics and accessibility.
The system shall provide alternative textual descriptions for all non-text media objects.
The system shall provide clear navigation indicators showing the user's current position within the application structure.
The system shall minimize the number of navigation steps required to reach specific content.
The system shall minimize vertical scrolling and avoid horizontal scrolling.
The system shall be designed for input device independence to allow operation via keyboard.
The application shall be designed to achieve acceptable download times across the expected range of network bandwidths.
The system shall support input and output of information using localized units of measurement, currency, and date formats.
The system shall provide a printer-friendly version for documents that are long or use complex layouts.
The system shall provide a site map showing the structure of the application.
The system shall be available to users during specified hours and weekdays with planned downtime not exceeding a defined threshold per three-month period.
The system shall be restorable within a specified number of hours following a software or hardware failure.
The system shall return search results for a simple search within 5-8 seconds.
The system shall return search results for an advanced search within 10-15 seconds.
The system shall be designed using a 3-tier architecture that separates presentation, business, and data-access logic.
The system shall support a multilingual user interface.
The system shall maintain operational data integrity in the event of equipment or communication network failure.
The system shall allow critical functionality to operate in offline mode.
The system shall use Open Standards and Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) principles.
The system shall use HTTPS over SSL for all network communications.
The system shall prevent SQL injection and cross-site scripting through input validation and parameterized queries.
The system shall perform only soft deletes by tagging rows for deletion instead of hard deletes.
The system shall fetch search results from the database in batches of 10 or 20 records.
